text: A 77-year-old man with metastatic prostate cancer reports worsening back pain. He is irritable and asks for medications exactly on time. Most likely explanation?
rationale: Dx: Undertreated pain. Risk: Irritability in cancer patients often suggests inadequate pain control[span_16](end_span)[span_17](end_span).

text: A 21-year-old man with status epilepticus (seizure >5 mins) is unresponsive. What is the most appropriate first-line pharmacotherapy?
rationale: Dx: GCSE (>5 min seizure). [span_18](start_span)[span_19](start_span)Tx: Benzodiazepines (e.g., lorazepam) are first-line[span_18](end_span)[span_19](end_span).

text: A 4-year-old boy has fever, sore throat, neck stiffness, and trismus (unable to open mouth). Likely diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Retropharyngeal abscess (RPA). [span_20](start_span)[span_21](start_span)Risk: Common in ages 2-4[span_20](end_span)[span_21](end_span).

text: A 56-year-old IV drug user has palpable purpura, arthralgia, and cryoglobulins. What is the next best test?
rationale: Dx: Cryoglobulinemic vasculitis. [span_22](start_span)[span_23](start_span)Tx: 90% associated with Hep C[span_22](end_span)[span_23](end_span).

text: A 30-year-old woman taking lithium is now pregnant. What is the specific fetal risk to discuss?
rationale: Risk: Lithium in pregnancy causes Ebstein anomaly (cardiac)[span_24](end_span)[span_25](end_span).

text: A 25-year-old female avoids social situations fearing scrutiny or embarrassment. Diagnosis and First-line Tx?
rationale: Dx: Social anxiety disorder. [span_26](start_span)[span_27](start_span)Tx: CBT is first-line[span_26](end_span)[span_27](end_span).

text: A patient on prochlorperazine feels restless and cannot stop moving his legs. Which med prevents this?
rationale: Dx: Akathisia. [span_28](start_span)[span_29](start_span)Tx: Prevent with IV benzodiazepines like lorazepam[span_28](end_span)[span_29](end_span).

text: 72yo with carotid bruit; Duplex shows complete occlusion of right ICA. Asymptomatic. Best management?
rationale: Tx: Medical therapy only. [span_30](start_span)[span_31](start_span)No benefit for CEA in complete occlusion[span_30](end_span)[span_31](end_span).

text: Which HTN medication has the highest risk of developing diabetes mellitus?
rationale: Risk: Thiazide diuretics (Chlorthalidone) increase glucose levels most[span_32](end_span)[span_33](end_span).

text: Diagnostic tests have higher Positive Predictive Value (PPV) than screening tests primarily because of:
rationale: Dx: PPV increases with higher disease prevalence in the tested population[span_34](end_span)[span_35](end_span).
